description_long: This object is a historical field cannon, featuring a long, cylindrical barrel mounted on a sturdy carriage with two large spoked wheels. The barrel is made of metal, likely cast iron or bronze, and is adorned with subtle decorative elements and reinforcing bands. The carriage is robust, with intricate relief patterns along its sides, and supports the barrel with a cradle and trunnions. The wheels are also metal, with thick spokes and a solid axle, designed to support the heavy weight of the cannon. The rear of the carriage is squared off, providing stability when firing, and the overall construction suggests durability and strength. The surface of the cannon and carriage shows a weathered, patinated finish, indicating age and exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered, metal field cannon with a long barrel and ornate carriage, mounted on two large spoked wheels. The surface is aged and patinated, with intricate relief details on the carriage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon barrel and the alignment of the two large spoked wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the barrel, ornate carriage details, and the profile of the spoked wheel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carriage, the breech of the barrel, and the wheel alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side of the barrel and carriage, with similar ornate details and wheel structure.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surface is a solid, weathered patina.
